explanation: The property expresses that whenever the ventilator is in the inspiration phase, it is always the case that the out valve is closed and the in valve pressure is set to the correct value depending on the mode: P_inspPCV for PCV mode, P_inspPSV for PSV mode, and P_inspAP for PCV from apnea backup mode. This is captured by stating that globally (at every time), if 'inspiratoryPhase' is true, then: (outValveClose is true) AND ( (if PCVMode then P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV) AND (if PSVMode then P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV) AND (if PCVMode & apnea then P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP) ). This ensures the correct commands/actions are asserted for all relevant modes whenever the ventilator is in the inspiration phase.

The requirement is: When the ventilator is in the inspiration state, the out valve must be closed AND the in valve pressure must be set to the target for the current mode. We focus on atomic propositions that capture these facts: 'inspiratoryPhase' indicates the inspiration state; 'outValveClose' indicates the out valve is closed; 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV'/'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV'/'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP' indicate the in-valve pressure is set per mode (PCV/PSV/AP backup). The correct invariant is: whenever we are in the inspiration phase, the out valve is closed AND one of the correct pressure targets is set. This can be represented as: G (inspiratoryPhase -> (outValveClose & (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP))). This matches template 30.

The requirement states that whenever the ventilator is in the inspiration phase (atomic proposition: inspiratoryPhase), the out valve must be closed (outValveClose is true), and the inspiratory pressure must be set to a target value, which varies according to the current mode:
- P_inspPCV if in PCV mode (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV when PCVMode)
- P_inspPSV if in PSV mode (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV when PSVMode)
- P_inspAP if in PCV from apnea backup (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP when in backup)

Because the atomic propositions map the setting of the inspiratory pressure, and outValveClose represents the closed out valve, we form the boolean expression for the desired state in the inspiratory phase as (outValveClose & (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP)) — capturing that the out valve is closed and the pressure is set to one of the targeted values (as necessary according to underlying mode). We want this to always hold while in the inspiratory phase.

Thus, we select the template: G (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3), where bool_exp2 is inspiratoryPhase (when in inspiration), and bool_exp3 is outValveClose & (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP).

The requirement states that whenever the ventilator is in the 'inspiration' phase, the out valve must be closed, and the in valve pressure must be set appropriately depending on the current mode (PCV, PSV, or PCV from apnea backup). In atomic propositions, 'inspiratoryPhase' represents being in an inspiration state, 'outValveClose' indicates the out valve is closed, and the target inspiratory pressures for each mode are given by 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV', 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV', and 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP'. The correct formalization should express: whenever 'inspiratoryPhase' is true, then both 'outValveClose' and at least one of the mode-matching pressure atoms is true (since the specific target depends on mode). Therefore, the LTL property is: G (inspiratoryPhase -> (outValveClose & (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP))). This uses template 30.

The requirement states that whenever the ventilator is in the inspiration phase (inspiratoryPhase), two things must always be true simultaneously: the expiratory valve (out valve) is closed (outValveClose), and the inspiratory valve pressure is set to the target value, which depends on the current ventilation mode. The three context statements (CONT39_1, ..._2, ..._3) specify which pressure is correct for each mode: in PCV, it should be P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV; in PSV, P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV; and in PCV from apnea backup, P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP. Therefore, the formalization is: whenever inspiratoryPhase holds, the out valve is closed, and at least one of the three mode-appropriate pressure signals holds true. This is best captured by a safety (globally) invariant, hence G (always), of the form: inspiratoryPhase -> (outValveClose & (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP)).

This LTL property ensures that whenever the ventilator is in the inspiratory phase, all of the following must hold: (1) the out valve is closed (outValveClose); and (2) the in-valve pressure is set to the appropriate target value depending on the ventilator mode: P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV if in PCV mode, P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV if in PSV mode, or P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP if in PCV mode for apnea backup. These are covered by the atomic propositions. The property requires that, globally (for all times), whenever 'inspiratoryPhase' is true, the out valve is closed, and at least one of the correct inspiratory pressures is set. This captures the requirement that in every inspiratory state, the out valve is closed and the in-valve pressure is set to the proper target, according to mode.

This property formalizes that whenever the system is in the inspiratory phase (represented by atomic proposition 'inspiratoryPhase'), then the following conditions must always hold: (1) the out valve is closed (outValveClose), (2) and the inspiratory pressure is set according to the current mode: P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV for PCV mode, P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV for PSV mode, or P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP for PCV mode under apnea backup. To capture this, the Boolean expression for 'bool_exp3' uses a disjunction of these three atomic propositions for pressure setting, reflecting that the requirement is satisfied if any one of them corresponding to the relevant mode is true in each inspiration phase. All the conditions are conjoined with outValveClose, and this implication is active during all occurrences of the inspiratoryPhase. Template 10 expresses a global ('G') property: always, if inspiratoryPhase then (outValveClose and (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP)). This directly matches the requirement that in every Inspiration state, those valve and pressure settings hold.

The property describes that whenever the ventilator is in the inspiratory phase, the out valve must be closed and the in valve pressure must correspond to the correct target, depending on the current mode. This is represented by the logical AND of being in inspiratoryPhase, the outValveClose being true (out valve is closed), and that one of the following must hold: (1) in PCV mode, the inspiratory valve pressure is set to P_inspPCV, (2) in PSV mode, pressure is set to P_inspPSV, or (3) if current mode is PCV from apnea backup, pressure is set to P_inspAP. The template expresses that this constraint holds globally, matching the specification's requirement for the whole duration of operation.
bool_exp3: inspiratoryPhase & outValveClose & ( ( PCVMode & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV ) | ( PSVMode & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV ) | ( (PCVMode & apnea) & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP ) )

explanation: The property describes that whenever the ventilator is in the inspiratory phase, the out valve must be closed and the in valve pressure is set to the correct target inspiratory pressure for the mode (PCV, PSV, or PCV from apnea backup). This is captured by combining (1) outValveClose is true (out valve is closed), and (2) P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV or P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV or P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP is true (in valve pressure is set appropriately). This needs to be true whenever inspiratoryPhase is active, hence a global implication (G (inspiratoryPhase -> (outValveClose & (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV | P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP)))).

The requirement states: When the ventilator is in Inspiration phase, the out valve should be closed and the in valve pressure should be set to the correct target (depending on the current ventilation mode: PCV, PSV, or apnea backup). This is best captured by an invariant that, whenever inspiratoryPhase is true, the following must hold: (1) Out valve is closed (i.e., NOT outValveOpen), and (2) The in-valve pressure is set to the correct mode target, represented by one of P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV (PCV mode), P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPSV (PSV mode), or P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP (apnea backup). Since only one of these targets should be used depending on the mode, the appropriate property is that at least one of them is set, i.e., a disjunction. Thus, the formula is an invariant: Always (if inspiratoryPhase, then outValveOpen is FALSE, and one of the in-valve pressures is set appropriately). The template G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3) is used, where bool_exp1 detects inspiratory phase and bool_exp3 is a conjunction of out valve closed and correct pressure set. N_DURATION is not used.
